description: The snake: the animal that is all curve and no limb, moving as water moves. A counted snake may take optional 'lipha'. Phi carries none of the snake's inherited villainy — it is simply the flowing one, reader of ground-warmth, shedder of skins.
sound_symbolism: Sinuous 's' into the liquid run of 'li'–'wa' — the word moves the way its animal does, without a single stop consonant to interrupt the glide.
grammatical_notes: Noun: 'suliwa mua kirua whenola.' (The snake wanders in the sand.) 'suliwa hisae leiro.' (The snake releases its skin.)

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: The shed skin is impermanence performed annually; the snake teaches letting-go by demonstration.
pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: The style's signature line — the whiplash curve — is the snake's path drawn; no animal is more Art Nouveau.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: The snake read the seasons before farmers did — first warmth, first shedding — and its skin-leaving made it the oldest emblem of renewal.
